---
title: 'S$\Omega$I: Score-based O-Information Estimation'
author:
  - name: Mustapha Bounoua
    affiliations:
      - EURECOM
      - Ampere Software Technology, France
  - name: Giulio Franzese
    affiliations:
      - EURECOM
  - name: Pietro Michiardi
    affiliations:
      - EURECOM
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    smaller: true
    controls: true
    transition: slide
    html-math-method: mathjax
bibliography: bibfile.bib
---

```{=html}
<script>
MathJax = {
  tex: {
    macros: {
      cT:    "\\mathcal{T}",
      cD:    "\\mathcal{D}",
      cI:    "\\mathcal{I}",
      norm:  ["\\left\\|#1\\right\\|", 1],
      E:     "\\mathbb{E}",
      indep: "\\perp\\!\\!\\!\\perp"
    }
  }
};
</script>
```

## Introduction

- Complex systems are often described by **multivariate** information
- Understanding the **relationships** among multiple random variables is crucial

::::: columns
::: {.column width="45%"}
![Brain regions](assets/brain.png){width="70%"}
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
![Sensors](assets/car.png){width="80%"}
:::
:::::

*How do the system components interact?*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Extensions of Shannon Mutual Information

- Shannon Mutual information: $\mathcal{I}(X^1; X^2)$
- Not interpretable for large systems $N>3$

::::: columns
::: {.column width="45%"}
**PID** [@williams2010nonnegative]:

- Requires a partition into sources and target
- Not scalable
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
**O-information** [@Rosas2019QuantifyingHI]:

- No partition needed
- Scalable
:::
:::::

. . .

> *SOTA is limited to discrete or Gaussian distributions*

- S$\Omega$I estimates O-information **without restrictions** on data type or **number** of variables

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Multivariate Interactions

::::: columns
::: {.column width="45%"}
***Redundancy***: The **shared** information between variables, recoverable from subsets
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
***Synergy***: Information arising from **jointly** observing all variables, inaccessible from individuals
:::
:::::

![](assets/figures/venn.png){width="90%"}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## High Dimensional Interaction Measures

Let $X = \{X^1,\dots,X^N\}$, $X^{>i} = \{X^{i+1},\dots,X^N\}$, $X^{\setminus i} = X \setminus \{X^i\}$.

**Total correlation:** $$\mathcal{T}(X) = \sum_{i=1}^{N} \mathcal{I}(X^i;\, X^{>i})$$ How much information $X^i$ **shares** with $X^{>i}$ — suggests *redundancy*

**Dual total correlation:** $$\mathcal{D}(X) = \sum_{i=1}^{N} \mathcal{I}(X^i;\, X^{\setminus i} \mid X^{<i})$$ How much **additional** information $X^i$ carries — suggests *synergy*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## O-Information

$$\boxed{\Omega(X) = \mathcal{T}(X) - \mathcal{D}(X)} \qquad
\begin{cases} \Omega(X)>0 & \text{Redundancy} \\ \Omega(X)<0 & \text{Synergy} \end{cases}$$

::::: columns
::: {.column width="65%"}
**Gradient of O-information** [@Scagliarini23]: $$\boxed{\partial_i \Omega(X) = \Omega(X) - \Omega(X^{\setminus i})}$$ Captures the individual influence of each variable
:::

::: {.column width="32%"}
:::
:::::

- Hard to estimate: **high dimensional** with arbitrary data **distribution**

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Score-based KL Divergence Estimation

Let ${\color{red}X_t} = {\color{green}X} + \sqrt{2t}\,{\color{red}W}$, $t\in[0,\infty)$. Using [@minde]:

$$\mathrm{KL}(p \| q) = \int p(x)\log\frac{p(x)}{q(x)}\,\mathrm{d}x
= \int p_t(x)\,\underbrace{\|\nabla\log p_t(x) - \nabla\log q_t(x)\|^2}_{\text{Difference of score functions}}\,\mathrm{d}x\,\mathrm{d}t$$

- Learn $\nabla\log p_t(\cdot)$ by denoising ${\color{red}X_t}$: $$\nabla\log p_t(x) = \frac{1}{2t}\Bigl(\underbrace{\mathbb{E}[X\mid{\color{red}X_t}]}_{\text{Denoiser}} - {\color{green}x}\Bigr)$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Score-based O-Information Estimation — TC

$$\mathcal{T}(X) = \mathrm{KL}\!\left(p(x)\,\Big\|\,\prod_{i=1}^N p(x^i)\right)
= \int \frac{1}{4t^2}\,\mathbb{E}\left\|
\mathbb{E}[X\mid{\color{red}X_t}] - \begin{bmatrix}\mathbb{E}[X^i\mid{\color{red}X^i_t}]\end{bmatrix}_{i=1}^N
\right\|^2\mathrm{d}t$$

- Compare denoiser output: all variables **together** (*Joint*) vs **separately** (*Marginals*)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Score-based O-Information Estimation — DTC

$$\mathcal{D}(X) = \int \frac{1}{4t^2}\,\mathbb{E}\left\|
\mathbb{E}[X\mid{\color{red}X_t}] -
\left[\mathbb{E}\!\left[X^i\mid{\color{red}X^i_t},\,{\color{green}X^{\setminus i}}\right]\right]_{i=1}^N
\right\|^2\mathrm{d}t$$

- Compare joint denoising vs individual denoising **conditioned** on remaining clean variables (*Conditionals*)

$$\Omega(X) = \mathcal{T}(X) - \mathcal{D}(X)$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Amortized Approach — Single Network

![Architecture](assets/archi.png){fig-align="center" width="70%"}

| Mode | Forward pass |
|------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
| **Joint** | $\epsilon_\theta([{\color{red}X_t^1,\dots,X_t^N}],\,t)$ |
| **Conditional** | $\epsilon_\theta([{\color{green}X^1,..,}{\color{red}X_t^i}{\color{green},..,X^N}],\,t)$ |
| **Marginal** | $\epsilon_\theta({\color{red}X_t^i},\,t)$ |

$$\hat\Omega(X) =
\underbrace{\frac{1}{4t^2}\left\|\epsilon(X_t)-[\epsilon(X_t^i)]_{i=1}^N\right\|^2}_{\hat{\mathcal{T}}(X)}
-
\underbrace{\frac{1}{4t^2}\left\|\epsilon(X_t)-[\epsilon(X_t^i\mid X^{\setminus i})]_{i=1}^N\right\|^2}_{\hat{\mathcal{D}}(X)}$$

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Experimental Validation

**Synthetic benchmark:**

- Multivariate Gaussian with/without transformation
- Redundancy, synergy, or a mix of both
- Varying number of variables, per-variable dimension, and interaction strength

**Baseline:** MI neural estimators

**Implementation:**

- *MLP* with skip connections — sufficient for simple settings
- *Transformer* — needed for gradient of O-information

**S**$\Omega$I efficiently estimates O-information across all challenging settings

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Synthetic Benchmarks — $\Omega(X)$

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="42%"}
**Redundancy:**\
$R \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{I})$

$$X = \{R + \varepsilon_i\}_{i=1}^N,\quad \varepsilon_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\sigma\mathbf{I})$$

**Synergy:**\
$S \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{I})$

$$X^1 \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{I}),\; X^2 = X^1+S,\; X^3 = S+\varepsilon$$

$\varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0,\sigma)$, $\;X^1 \perp X^3$
:::

:::: {.column width="55%"}
![](assets/figures/exp_red/legend.PNG){width="65%" fig-align="center"}

![](assets/figures/exp_red/plot_red_0_dim_20.jpg){width="65%" fig-align="center"}

![](assets/figures/exp_red/plot_syn_0_dim_20.jpg){width="65%" fig-align="center"}

::: {style="font-size:0.75em;text-align:center;color:#555;margin-top:0.3em;"}
10 variables, dim=20. **Top:** Redundancy. **Bottom:** Synergy.
:::
::::
::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Synthetic Benchmarks — $\partial\Omega(X)$

::::: columns
::: {.column width="48%"}
![](assets/figures/grad/grad_tx_setting_0_dim_20.png){width="100%"}

```{=html}
<p style="text-align:center;font-size:0.8em;color:#555;margin-top:0.3em;">
  6-variable mixed system
  <span style="color:#4a90d9;">&#9632;</span> redundancy &nbsp;
  <span style="color:#e07b3a;">&#9632;</span> synergy
</p>
```
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
![](assets/figures/grad/grad_tx_setting_1_dim_20.png){width="100%"}

```{=html}
<p style="text-align:center;font-size:0.8em;color:#555;margin-top:0.3em;">
  10-variable mixed system
  <span style="color:#4a90d9;">&#9632;</span> redundancy &nbsp;
  <span style="color:#e07b3a;">&#9632;</span> synergy
</p>
```
:::
:::::

::: {style="font-size:0.75em;color:#666;margin-top:0.8em;"}
*Results with transformer-based architecture.*
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## O-Information in the Mice Brain [@venkatesh2024gaussian]

::::: columns
::: {.column width="42%"}
![](assets/figures/vbn.png)

Visual Behaviour Neuropixels (VBN). Spike recordings from 6 mouse brain regions. S$\Omega$I estimates O-information per 50 ms bin after stimulus flash.
:::

::: {.column width="55%"}
![6 brain regions](assets/figures/vbn/new_vbn_setting_6_dim_25.png){width="90%"}

Higher **redundant** information in visual cortex is transmitted after a flash with a new scene
:::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Conclusion

- S$\Omega$I captures multivariate interactions for **any data distribution** and **large number of variables** — no restriction to discrete or Gaussian

- The only required ingredient is **the score function** — extends to images, audio, fMRI, multimodal data, etc.

- S$\Omega$I opens the door to many **scientific applications** *(open for collaboration!)*

::: {style="text-align:right;margin-top:1.5em;"}
[![Project repo](assets/qrcode.png){width="12%"}](https://mustaphabounoua.github.io/soi_o_inf/)\
[mustaphabounoua.github.io/soi_o_inf](https://mustaphabounoua.github.io/soi_o_inf/)
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

##  {.center}

::: {style="text-align:center;font-size:2.2em;font-weight:bold;margin-top:1.5em;"}
Thank you!
:::

::: {style="text-align:center;margin-top:2.5em;"}
:::

## References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
